Title: Total Biosynthesis of the Tubulin-Binding Alkaloid Colchicine
Journal: Journal of the American Chemical Society

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana via pEAQ-HT; promoter not specified in provided text
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as precursor-supply engineering module to raise colchicine yield in heterologous systems; can be stacked with full pathway genes to improve flux from primary metabolism, but alone does not define pathway specificity.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases phenylalanine precursor supply and thereby boosts downstream colchicine alkaloid yield in engineered Nicotiana benthamiana.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In planta metabolic engineering context; pathway reconstruction with added precursor-supply enzyme improved alkaloid production.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana under 16:8 light:dark growth; substrate/product accumulation measured by LC-MS.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: not reported for G. superba because enzyme is heterologous
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptomics used for G. superba candidate discovery; no accession reported for this heterologous gene in provided text
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This enzyme was one of three non-G. superba enzymes used in the 20-gene total biosynthesis platform; it supports precursor supply rather than a dedicated colchicine-specific tailoring step.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential redirection of aromatic amino acid flux and broader metabolic perturbation in engineered hosts; no specific growth penalty reported.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpADT-A was added to the reconstructed pathway to increase phenylalanine precursor supply. 2. Its inclusion improved downstream colchicine pathway yield in N. benthamiana. 3. It serves as a flux-enhancing auxiliary gene rather than a dedicated colchicine-branch enzyme.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: distributed in G. superba tissues; engineered production in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tubulin-binding cytotoxic defense compound and pharmaceutical anti-inflammatory/antimitotic agent.

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana via pEAQ-HT; promoter not specified in provided text
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can substitute for a missing native tyrosine 3'-hydroxylation step during pathway engineering; practical for complete heterologous colchicine production until a native equivalent is identified.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides L-Tyr 3'-hydroxylation activity during engineered dopamine supply, enabling total colchicine biosynthesis in N. benthamiana.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway reconstruction and transient expression in N. benthamiana; functional use in engineered pathway based on prior biochemical confirmation of activity.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana under standard growth conditions; metabolite outputs measured by LC-MS.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Cytochrome P450 reductase (host endogenous or coexpressed redox partner required generally for CYP activity)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptomics used to search for native G. superba candidates; no accession reported for this heterologous gene in provided text
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used because no tested G. superba CYP catalyzed L-Tyr 3'-hydroxylation. Authors contrast it with GsPPO1, which instead hydroxylates tyramine to dopamine.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Heterologous CYP may impose redox burden and alter host tyrosine-derived metabolism; no specific phenotype reported.
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Likely endoplasmic reticulum/microsomal membrane as a plant CYP
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvCYP76AD5 was included in the heterologous pathway because it is a biochemically confirmed plant L-Tyr 3'-hydroxylase. 2. It supplied early dopamine-pathway chemistry required for total colchicine biosynthesis. 3. Its use highlights that the equivalent native G. superba enzyme remains unresolved.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: distributed in G. superba tissues; engineered production in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tubulin-binding cytotoxic defense compound and pharmaceutical anti-inflammatory/antimitotic agent.

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana via pEAQ-HT; promoter not specified in provided text
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful heterologous module to bypass unidentified native Pictet-Spenglerase and complete engineered colchicine biosynthesis; can be replaced by native enzyme when discovered.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes the Pictet-Spengler condensation between dopamine and 4-hydroxydihydrocinnamaldehyde, enabling formation of the initial phenethylisoquinoline scaffold feeding colchicine biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional use in reconstructed N. benthamiana pathway for total colchicine biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana; metabolites assayed by LC-MS.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptomics used for G. superba candidate discovery; no accession reported for this heterologous gene in provided text
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This heterologous enzyme was required because the native G. superba Pictet-Spenglerase remains unidentified and may belong to a novel family.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May generate stereochemical or side-product considerations in heterologous hosts, though successful production of native (-)-colchicine was achieved overall.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CjNCS was used to catalyze scaffold-forming Pictet-Spengler condensation in the engineered pathway. 2. It fills a missing native step during heterologous reconstitution of colchicine biosynthesis. 3. Discovery of the native G. superba counterpart remains an open problem.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: distributed in G. superba tissues; engineered production in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tubulin-binding cytotoxic defense compound and pharmaceutical anti-inflammatory/antimitotic agent.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana via pEAQ-HT; promoter not specified in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value terminal tailoring enzyme for heterologous colchicine production; should be stacked with GsABH1 and upstream pathway genes. Likely suitable target for expression optimization because it enables committed late-stage nitrogen remodeling specific to colchicine formation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes the N-methyl group of N-formyldemecolcine (3) to a hemiaminal intermediate (3'), which then nonenzymatically decomposes with formaldehyde loss to form N-formyldeacetylcolchicine (4a).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes conversion of pathway intermediate 3 into 4a, establishing N-demethylation as the first of the final three steps toward colchicine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Correlation-guided candidate identification; Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana with substrate feeding; yeast microsomal in vitro assay; LC-MS/MS detection of substrate, hemiaminal intermediate, and product.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate downstream intermediate leading directly toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Reaction is NADPH dependent; active in rhizome microsomal fractions; tested in N. benthamiana leaves and yeast microsomes.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher relative expression in leaf than most upstream colchicine genes; part of final four genes with distinct expression pattern; detected across transcriptomes and associated with late pathway module.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Same as applied species (native G. superba gene): elevated in leaf relative to upstream pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Cytochrome P450 reductase; GsABH1 functionally coupled in two-enzyme system though direct interaction not demonstrated
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: G. superba RNA-seq transcriptomics (in-house plus public transcriptomes); expression correlation analysis; no sequence-read accession provided in text
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No activity detected with demecolcine (4b). The direct enzymatic product is likely unstable hemiaminal 3', not 4a itself. Authors infer canonical CYP N-demethylation chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late nitrogen-tailoring module after tropolone formation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct tradeoff reported; as a CYP it may require redox capacity and could potentially create unstable intermediates if uncoupled from downstream steps.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Hemiaminal intermediate (3'), which decomposes to N-formyldeacetylcolchicine (4a)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key late-pathway control step inferred; decisive for initiating final nitrogen-tailoring sequence but not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsCYP71DA12 was identified by correlation analysis using GsCYP71FB1 as query. 2. It catalyzes NADPH-dependent N-demethylation of 3 via a hydroxylated hemiaminal intermediate. 3. This establishes that in G. superba N-demethylation precedes N-deformylation in the major route to colchicine.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Colchicine accumulates in rhizome, root, stem, and leaf; engineered production in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tubulin-binding cytotoxic compound implicated in plant defense and used pharmaceutically.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana via pEAQ-HT; promoter not specified in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential late-pathway enzyme for heterologous colchicine assembly; best deployed together with GsCYP71DA12 to ensure generation of its preferred unstable substrate and avoid bottlenecks.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolytic N-deformylation of the CYP-generated intermediate, likely acting preferentially on the hemiaminal 3' rather than stable 4a, to yield N-deacetylcolchicine (5).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables conversion of late nitrogen-tailored intermediate toward the immediate colchicine precursor 5.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression-guided candidate testing; transient coexpression in N. benthamiana with in situ substrate generation; in vitro assays with purified protein plus GsCYP71DA12 microsomes; LC-MS detection of 5.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor to target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Reaction is NADPH independent for deformylation itself but requires GsCYP71DA12 and NADPH to generate preferred substrate 3'; assayed in plant lysates and N. benthamiana transient system.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher relative expression in leaf than most upstream pathway genes; part of final four late-pathway genes with distinct expression pattern.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Same as applied species (native G. superba gene).
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Functional coupling with GsCYP71DA12 in tandem assays; no direct physical interaction proven
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: late-pathway step immediately before terminal acetylation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: G. superba RNA-seq transcriptomics (in-house plus public transcriptomes); correlation analysis used for candidate selection; no sequence-read accession provided in text
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Purified GsABH1 showed no activity on 4a alone in vitro, suggesting the true substrate is unstable hemiaminal 3'. Low activity on 3 to 4b and on 4a in planta was detectable but likely nonpreferred/suboptimal.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late nitrogen-tailoring module after tropolone formation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: If expressed without upstream CYP, may show little productive flux due to substrate mismatch; no organismal tradeoff reported.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Likely hemiaminal intermediate (3'); low activity observed with 4a and 3 in some contexts
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting; functions as necessary intermediate-processing step with substrate-channeling-like dependence
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsABH1 was identified among coexpressed alpha/beta hydrolases. 2. It supports N-deformylation in the late colchicine pathway, likely preferring the unstable hemiaminal 3' generated by GsCYP71DA12. 3. Its activity completes formation of the direct precursor 5 before final acetylation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Colchicine accumulates in rhizome, root, stem, and leaf; engineered production in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tubulin-binding cytotoxic compound implicated in plant defense and used pharmaceutically.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana via pEAQ-HT; promoter not specified in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Terminal enzyme for direct colchicine formation; highly useful engineering target to complete pathway and should be included in any production chassis. Specificity for 5 may reduce side reactions.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetyl group from acetyl-CoA to the primary amine of N-deacetylcolchicine (5) to form colchicine (1).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly produces colchicine from the immediate precursor 5.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate discovery by coexpression; transient expression in N. benthamiana with substrate feeding; in vitro assays with purified enzyme; LC-MS quantification and kinetic analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Acetyl-CoA dependent; assayed in transient plant expression and purified enzyme reactions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher relative expression in leaf than most upstream pathway genes; one of the final four late genes with distinct tissue distribution.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Same as applied species (native G. superba gene).
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: G. superba RNA-seq transcriptomics (in-house plus public transcriptomes); candidate selected by correlation analysis; no sequence-read accession provided in text
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific toward 5 in tested assays; no activity with demecolcine (4b) or tyramine. NAT family involvement broadens known plant specialized metabolism chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late nitrogen-tailoring module after tropolone formation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could consume acetyl-CoA in engineered systems; no growth penalty reported.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for colchicine formation; direct product-forming step, but not explicitly claimed rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsNAT1 was the sole strongly correlated NAT candidate identified across both transcriptomes. 2. It efficiently acetylates 5 to produce colchicine in planta and in vitro. 3. The three-enzyme set GsCYP71DA12, GsABH1, and GsNAT1 completes the final missing steps of colchicine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Colchicine accumulates in rhizome, root, stem, and leaf; engineered production in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tubulin-binding cytotoxic compound implicated in plant defense and used pharmaceutically.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana via pEAQ-HT; promoter not specified in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising native alternative to heterologous BvCYP76AD5 for installing dopamine supply in engineered colchicine pathways; may be useful in plant alkaloid engineering more broadly.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies dopamine precursor chemistry compatible with early colchicine biosynthesis in G. superba.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcript coexpression analysis; transient expression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS/HILIC analysis of tyramine-to-dopamine conversion.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assayed in N. benthamiana transient expression with polar metabolite LC-MS analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Correlates with GsTyDC/DDC (Pearson r > 0.7); detailed tissue pattern not otherwise specified.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Same as applied species (native G. superba gene).
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GsTyDC/DDC is the functionally linked upstream/downstream enzyme in dopamine branch
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: G. superba RNA-seq transcriptomics (in-house plus public transcriptomes); correlation analysis with GsTyDC/DDC; no sequence-read accession provided in text
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Distinct from BvCYP76AD5, which acts preferentially on L-Tyr to L-DOPA. Supports speculation that plant PPOs may broadly participate in dopamine-related metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Dopamine-forming branch feeding phenethylisoquinoline scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter broader phenolic/amine metabolism due to PPO-related activities; no direct tradeoff measured.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsPPO1 was identified as the only PPO coexpressed with GsTyDC/DDC. 2. It catalyzes tyramine hydroxylation to dopamine in the transient expression system. 3. It provides a plausible native route for early dopamine formation in colchicine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Colchicine accumulates in rhizome, root, stem, and leaf
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tubulin-binding cytotoxic compound implicated in plant defense and used pharmaceutically.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: not experimentally applied in this paper for direct validation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker/query gene for mining downstream pathway genes; previously identified tropolone-forming enzyme relevant for complete pathway stacking.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes tropolone-ring formation in upstream colchicine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for formation of the tropolone scaffold precursor module upstream of final nitrogen-tailoring steps.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previously characterized pathway enzyme referenced in this study; expression correlation analysis used here as discovery handle.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms characteristic scaffold required for target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: not specifically examined in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: One of final four genes showing relatively high expression in leaf versus other upstream pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: late-mid pathway upstream of terminal nitrogen tailoring
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: G. superba RNA-seq transcriptomics used for correlation analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its weaker correlation with earlier genes prompted the strategy that discovered final pathway enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsCYP71FB1 is a previously characterized tropolone-forming enzyme in colchicine biosynthesis. 2. Its atypical coexpression pattern relative to other genes hinted at distinct regulation of downstream steps. 3. Using it as a query enabled discovery of GsCYP71DA12 and other late-pathway candidates.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Colchicine accumulates in rhizome, root, stem, and leaf
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tubulin-binding cytotoxic compound implicated in plant defense and used pharmaceutically.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: not experimentally applied in this paper for direct validation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Previously validated upstream pathway enzyme; useful component for full pathway reconstruction though not newly characterized here.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Previously characterized oxidative tailoring step in colchicine biosynthesis; exact reaction not described in provided text.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports upstream formation of colchicine intermediates leading to compound 3.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced as previously characterized pathway gene; identified again by correlation analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Previously characterized coexpressed pathway gene; detailed pattern not given here.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: G. superba RNA-seq transcriptomics used for correlation analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned among CYPs coexpressing with GsCYP71FB1.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsCYP75A109 is a previously characterized colchicine pathway CYP. 2. It coappeared in correlation analysis with GsCYP71FB1. 3. It belongs to the established upstream pathway module leading toward 3.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Colchicine accumulates in rhizome, root, stem, and leaf
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tubulin-binding cytotoxic compound implicated in plant defense and used pharmaceutically.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: not experimentally applied in this paper for direct validation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Previously validated upstream pathway enzyme; useful component for full pathway reconstruction though not newly characterized here.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Previously characterized oxidative tailoring step in colchicine biosynthesis; exact reaction not described in provided text.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports upstream formation of colchicine intermediates leading to compound 3.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced as previously characterized pathway gene; identified again by correlation analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Previously characterized coexpressed pathway gene; detailed pattern not given here.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: G. superba RNA-seq transcriptomics used for correlation analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned among CYPs coexpressing with GsCYP71FB1.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsCYP75A110 is a previously characterized colchicine pathway CYP. 2. It coappeared in correlation analysis with GsCYP71FB1. 3. It belongs to the established upstream pathway module leading toward 3.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Colchicine accumulates in rhizome, root, stem, and leaf
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tubulin-binding cytotoxic compound implicated in plant defense and used pharmaceutically.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: not experimentally applied in this paper for direct validation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports early precursor branch engineering because it can decarboxylate either L-Tyr or L-DOPA, making it compatible with alternative hydroxylation sequences.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates L-Tyr and/or L-DOPA, supporting formation of tyramine and/or dopamine precursor branch feeding colchicine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides flexibility in the route to dopamine precursor supply for the colchicine pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced from prior work and discussed in relation to GsPPO1/BvCYP76AD5 route logic.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Used as coexpression anchor with GsPPO1; exact tissue pattern not detailed here.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GsPPO1 functionally linked in dopamine branch
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: G. superba RNA-seq transcriptomics used in correlation analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its dual substrate tolerance makes tyramine hydroxylase activity of GsPPO1 plausible within native pathway context.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could alter aromatic amine pools in engineering contexts; no direct tradeoff reported.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Tyramine and/or dopamine depending on substrate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GsTyDC/DDC is an early branch enzyme previously implicated in colchicine biosynthesis. 2. It can act on either L-Tyr or L-DOPA, enabling alternative precursor routes. 3. Its expression correlation helped identify GsPPO1 as a native tyramine hydroxylase candidate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Colchicine accumulates in rhizome, root, stem, and leaf
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tubulin-binding cytotoxic compound implicated in plant defense and used pharmaceutically.
